intro: Cronquistianthus pseudoriganoides, also known as a wild or Mexican sage, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to act as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Cronquistianthus pseudoriganoides include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cronquistianthus pseudoriganoides is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of ailments, including stress, fever, and respiratory issues.
